question: Does the appreciation model generalize beyond the qualities it was trained on?

zero_shot_to_never_trained_tokens.setup: Hold qualities out of TRAINING entirely; their words are only seeded into the tokenizer (so they aren't <unk>) but the model never sees them in a training window.
zero_shot_to_never_trained_tokens.why: A held-out quality's token embedding receives no gradient, so a tied-embedding model cannot emit it; the model substitutes a trained quality instead (e.g. 'Topic: wisdom' -> 'your calmness ...'). This is a fundamental limitation of word-level embeddings, not a tuning failure. It held at both 24-quality and 205-quality training scales.
zero_shot_to_never_trained_tokens.honest_conclusion: True zero-shot generalization to a never-trained token is ~0% for this architecture.

coverage_generalization.setup: Train on a much larger, diverse quality vocabulary so every quality the model is asked about is trained.
coverage_generalization.why: Expanding from 24 to 229 wholesome, vetted single-word qualities (brainstormed across 12 categories + critic-vetted) makes the model generalize across a 10x larger, diverse quality space — all 229 produce valid, wholesome appreciation. This is the achievable, useful form of generalization for a tiny word-level model.

examples.candor: your candor inspired the rest of us.
examples.grit: i really appreciate your grit. it caught the problems early.
examples.poise: thank you for your poise. it gave us room to get it right.
examples.stewardship: i am grateful for your stewardship. it made someone's day.

note: Measured, not claimed. The shipped model erosolar-v0.01 trains on all 229 qualities. Both findings are reported honestly; neither is a capability-class claim.
